text.title: ClickUp Automations and Integrations
text.summary: Native automation patterns, GitHub/Slack/Toggl integration setup, and automation governance rules.

rule.statement: Use native ClickUp automations for mechanical flows before building agent-driven automations: auto-assign on status change, notify on overdue, create subtasks from template on task creation.
rule.rationale: Native automations run instantly, do not consume API rate limit, and require no infrastructure; agents should handle logic that native automations cannot express.

rule.statement: Document all active automations in a shared "Automation Registry" list inside the workspace; review monthly for unused or conflicting automations.
rule.rationale: Automation loops (trigger A fires action that triggers B which fires action back to A) cause infinite execution and exhaust the monthly automation limit; a registry makes circular dependencies visible.

example.note: Automation: "When status changes to In Progress → assign Developer" + automation "When Developer assigned → set status to In Progress" — these two automations loop infinitely.

rule.statement: GitHub integration: set branch naming to "clickup-{task_id}-{task_name}" and enable auto-status-on-merge to "Complete"; link PRs to tasks via branch name convention.
rule.rationale: Consistent branch naming enables bidirectional traceability between code and tasks; auto-status on merge eliminates manual "mark as done" steps.

rule.statement: Archive tasks (set to "Won't Do") rather than deleting — deleted tasks cannot be recovered and break any rollup or reporting that referenced them.
rule.rationale: ClickUp has no bulk undo; deletions are permanent. Won't Do status keeps historical data intact and preserves rollup counts.

section.title: API Rate Limits

rule.statement: ClickUp API v2: use Authorization header with token directly (no "Bearer" prefix); implement exponential backoff on 429 responses; bulk custom field updates require one POST per task per field.
rule.rationale: The non-standard Authorization header format is a frequent agent bug; 100 req/min limit is hit quickly during workspace bootstrap; no bulk custom field endpoint exists.

section.reference: Prompt for agent to create workspace hierarchy via API
